lemma_statement.name: restricted_continuation_boundary_flux_sign_control_discharge_lemma
lemma_statement.claim: On the restricted-continuation boundary portion partial Sigma_R, the certified boundary flux pairing has the required sign control whenever the explicitly listed outward-orientation, flux-sign, and restricted-boundary compatibility hypotheses are supplied.

required_inputs.name: restricted_continuation_boundary_flux_pairing_exists
required_inputs.role: supplies the certified boundary flux pairing over partial Sigma_R

required_inputs.name: outward_normal_orientation_matches_energy_identity
required_inputs.role: ensures the sign convention in the boundary pairing matches the energy identity convention

required_inputs.name: flux_payload_has_boundary_dissipativity_sign
required_inputs.role: supplies the sign condition needed to control the boundary flux contribution

required_inputs.name: restricted_boundary_support_excludes_uncontrolled_faces
required_inputs.role: ensures no uncontrolled boundary portion contributes to the sign-controlled flux term

required_inputs.name: boundary_flux_sign_control_operator_available
required_inputs.role: supplies the certified map from flux-pairing data to the required sign-control statement

discharged_obligations: Separates boundary flux sign control from boundary flux pairing existence.
Certifies the exact assumptions under which the restricted-continuation boundary flux pairing has the required sign.
Promotes boundary flux sign control from an undischarged target obligation to a conditional discharge lemma.
non_claims: Does not prove restricted integration by parts.
Does not prove the energy inequality.
Does not prove continuation or contradiction.
Does not prove full RR closure.
